title: Order 2023/5 of 16 November 2023
text:
INTERNATIONAL TRIBUNAL FOR THE LAW OF THE SEA
REPORTS OF JUDGMENTS,
ADVISORY OPINIONS AND ORDERS
THE M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (No. 2) CASE
(MARSHALL ISLANDS/EQUATORIAL GUINEA)

ORDER OF 16 NOVEMBER 2023
2023
TRIBUNAL INTERNATIONAL DU DROIT DE LA MER

AVIS CONSULTATIFS ET ORDONNANCES

16 NOVEMBER 2023
ORDER
THE M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (No. 2) CASE
(MARSHALL ISLANDS/EQUATORIAL GUINEA)
AFFAIRE DU NAVIRE « HEROIC IDUN » (No. 2)
(ÎLES MARSHALL/GUINÉE ÉQUATORIALE)
16 NOVEMBRE 2023
ORDONNANCE

231
INTERNATIONAL TRIBUNAL FOR THE LAW OF THE SEA
YEAR 2023
16 November 2023
List of cases:
No. 32
THE M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (No. 2) CASE
(MARSHALL ISLANDS/EQUATORIAL GUINEA)
ORDER
The President of the Special Chamber of the International Tribunal for the
Law of the Sea formed to deal with the above-mentioned case under article 15,
paragraph 2, of the Statute of the Tribunal (hereinafter “the Statute”),
Having regard to article 27 of the Statute,
Having regard to article 59, paragraphs 2 and 3, and article 107 of the Rules
of the Tribunal,
Having regard to the Order of the President of the Special Chamber of
19 May 2023,
Makes the following Order:
1. Whereas the President of the Special Chamber, by his Order dated 19 May
2023, fixed 20 November 2023 and 20 May 2024 as the respective time-limits
for the filing of the Memorial of the Republic of the Marshall Islands (here-
inafter “the Marshall Islands”) and the Counter-Memorial of the Republic of
Equatorial Guinea (hereinafter “Equatorial Guinea”);

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 16 NOVEMBER 2023) 232
2. Whereas, by letter dated 9 November 2023, the Agent of the Marshall
Islands requested that the time-limit fixed for the submission of the Memorial
be extended until 18 December 2023 and that a corresponding adjustment be
made as regards the time-limit for the submission of the Counter-Memorial of
Equatorial Guinea;
3. Whereas, in the same letter, the Agent of the Marshall Islands provided
justification in support of the request of the Marshall Islands for an extension
of the time-limit for the submission of its Memorial;
4. Whereas, by letter of the Registrar of 10 November 2023 transmitting the
request of the Marshall Islands, the Agent of Equatorial Guinea was invited
to communicate the views of Equatorial Guinea on the said request not later
than 15 November 2023, noon, and whereas no response was received from
Equatorial Guinea;
5. Being satisfied that there is adequate justification for the request for an
extension of the time-limit,
THE PRESIDENT OF THE SPECIAL CHAMBER
Having ascertained the views of the Parties,
Extends the time-limit for the submission of the Memorial of the Marshall
Islands to 18 December 2023;
Extends the time-limit for the submission of the Counter-Memorial of
Equatorial Guinea to 15 July 2024;
Reserves the subsequent procedure for further decision.
Done in English and in French, both texts being equally authoritative, in
the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg, this sixteenth day of November, two
thousand and twenty-three, in three copies, one of which will be placed in the
archives of the Tribunal and the others transmitted to the Government of the
Marshall Islands and to the Government of Equatorial Guinea, respectively.

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 16 NOVEMBER 2023) 233
(signed)
Albert J. Hoffmann,
President of the Special Chamber
(signed)
Ximena Hinrichs Oyarce,
Registrar
court: International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)
